ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: High quality text-based PDF. Original certificate on page 1, Copy on page 2. Page 3 contains a copy of the invoice embedded in the certificate file.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: High quality text-based PDF. Single page invoice issued by Bulten Sweden AB. Identical content to page 3 of the certificate file.

document_pairing.method: Certificate file (Document A, pages 1-2) identified as certificate. Invoice file (Document B, page 1) identified as invoice. Page 3 of Document A is an embedded invoice copy and is NOT used for verification per rule 8.5.0.

consignor.certificate_value: Bulten Sweden AB, Box512, SE-734 27, HALLSTAHAMMAR, Sweden
consignor.invoice_value: Bulten Sweden AB, Box 512, SE-734 27 Hallstahammar, Sweden (footer center block); Bulten Sweden AB, Box 9148, 400 93 Göteborg, Sweden (footer left block)

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name 'Bulten Sweden AB' anywhere in invoice file
comparison_steps.result: FOUND – 'Bulten Sweden AB' appears in invoice footer left block, footer center block, and header branding 'BULTEN'. Name is an exact match after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Verify country Sweden for consignor in invoice
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – Invoice footer explicitly states 'Sweden' for both Bulten Sweden AB addresses. VAT number SE556078364801 has SE prefix confirming Sweden.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found explicitly in invoice file
Land verification via VAT prefix SE and explicit country in address

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Bulten Sweden AB, Box512, SE-734 27, HALLSTAHAMMAR, Sweden'. Invoice issuer identified as 'Bulten Sweden AB' in footer blocks (Box 9148, Göteborg and Box 512, Hallstahammar, both Sweden). Company name is identical after normalization. Country Sweden confirmed via explicit address 'Sweden' and VAT prefix 'SE' in SE556078364801. Rule 4.1.0 gives MATCH since name is found explicitly in invoice. Address difference (Box 512 Hallstahammar in cert vs Box 9148 Göteborg as primary in invoice) is irrelevant per 4.1.5 – only name and country matter. The invoice footer center block also shows Box 512, SE-734 27 Hallstahammar, Sweden – matching certificate address exactly.

consignee.certificate_value: Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S, Fatih Mahallesi Gölyolu no:26, 16800 Orhangazi - Bursa, Turkey
consignee.invoice_value: Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S., Fatih Mahallesi Gölyolu no:26, 16800 Orhangazi - Bursa, Turkey (Payer field)

comparison_steps.description: Invoice 'Payer' field identifies 'Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S.' as the billed party.
comparison_steps.result: FOUND – Certificate consignee matches invoice Payer field.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S' (no trailing period). Invoice: 'Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S.' (with trailing period). Difference is only punctuation.
Certificate consignee country: Turkey. Invoice Payer address: Turkey.

rules_applied.description: Consignee identified in primary billing field (Payer)

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S' at 'Fatih Mahallesi Gölyolu no:26, 16800 Orhangazi - Bursa, Turkey'. Invoice Payer field: 'Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S.' at same address in Turkey. The only difference is a trailing period after 'A.S' vs 'A.S.' which is a punctuation normalization. Company name, address and country all match exactly. The Delivery address field also shows the same company and address, further confirming the match.

goods_description.invoice_value:
M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB
Screws, with head, hexagon, class >=8.8
Customs Stat No 73181588

comparison_steps.description: Certificate does not contain a standard article number (the stat no 73181588 is a customs statistical number, not an article/item number). Certificate does not reference item numbers like 2000048001F1 or 991179. Priority 1 not applicable for article number matching.
comparison_steps.result: NOT_APPLICABLE – no article number in certificate

comparison_steps.description: Identify identity-bearing main designation in certificate: 'M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB'. Search for this exact string in invoice.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – Invoice line item 1 description contains 'M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB' exactly.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate also mentions 'Fasteners' and 'Stat no 73181588'. Invoice mentions 'Screws, with head, hexagon, class >=8.8' and Customs Stat No 73181588. 'Fasteners' is a general category consistent with 'Screws'. Stat no 73181588 matches Customs Stat No 73181588. These are additional descriptors that do not change the product identity.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation 'M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB' found exactly in invoice
Non-identity-bearing additions – customs stat no and general category consistent

goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate goods description contains: '6 pallets Fasteners / M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB / Stat no 73181588'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB' – this is the specific product identifier combining size (M14x90), type (STUD), grade (8.8) and coating (Zi/NiB). This exact string is found in invoice line 1: 'M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB'. The customs statistical number 73181588 matches exactly. 'Fasteners' in certificate is consistent with 'Screws, with head, hexagon, class >=8.8' in invoice as a general product category (fasteners encompass screws). Per rule 4.3.4, additional descriptors that do not change product identity are not grounds for MISMATCH. No article number is specified in the certificate for Priority 1 matching. Result: MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because certificate says 'Fasteners' and invoice says 'Screws'
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: 'Fasteners' appears in the certificate as a general goods description alongside the specific identity-bearing designation 'M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB'. The specific designation matches exactly. Per 4.3.4, additional category labels that do not change product identity are not grounds for MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 contains '1674 kg Gross'. This is the verification-grounding quantity. Numeric value: 1674. Unit: kg. Weight category: Gross.
Search for numeric value 1674 in invoice weight fields. Invoice states 'Gross weight (kg) 1674' in the summary section and also in the Customs Stat No breakdown row 'Gross weight (kg) 1674'.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 1674 found as Gross weight (kg) in invoice

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'kg Gross' → GW. Invoice: 'Gross weight (kg) 1674' → GW. Same weight category.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.3.1 – Check for contradictions with other quantities
comparison_steps.description: Invoice also shows Net weight (kg) 1488. Certificate only states GW 1674. No NW is stated in certificate. No contradiction – certificate does not claim NW.

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check – numeric value 1674 found in invoice Gross weight field
Weight category – both state Gross Weight in kg

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: '1674 kg Gross'. This is the verification-grounding quantity per 4.4.2.1. Numeric value 1674, unit kg, category Gross Weight. Invoice explicitly states 'Gross weight (kg) 1674' in both the summary area and the customs breakdown row. Exact match: 1674 = 1674. Unit: kg = kg. Category: Gross = Gross weight. Additionally, certificate box 6 mentions '6 pallets' which matches invoice 'No of pallets 6' – but the primary verification is via box 7 GW. No contradictions found. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice. Invoice has an 'Origin' column in the line item table. Line item 1 shows 'SE' in the Origin column.
Certificate: 'Sweden'. Invoice: 'SE'. SE is the ISO alpha-2 code for Sweden. Normalization of full country name vs ISO code is expressly permitted.

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'Sweden'. Invoice Origin column for line 1: 'SE'. SE is the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for Sweden. Per rule 4.5.4, normalization of full country name vs ISO code is permitted. Sweden = SE → MATCH. No other origin countries are mentioned in either document. Single origin, single article – no mixed origin complications.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter överensstämmer. Avsändaren Bulten Sweden AB i Sverige kan identifieras i fakturan, mottagaren Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S. i Turkiet matchar fakturans betalare, varubeskrivningen M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB är identisk, bruttovikten 1674 kg överensstämmer, och ursprungslandet Sverige bekräftas av landskod SE i fakturans ursprungsfält. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: All five checkpoints (consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity, country of origin) verified as MATCH. Certificate No. SEG-25D-340444 issued 2025-08-26 for invoice 823184. Consignor Bulten Sweden AB (Sweden) identified in invoice issuer fields. Consignee Döktas Dökümcülük Tic. ve San. A.S. (Turkey) matches invoice Payer. Goods 'M14x90 STUD 8.8 Zi/NiB' with Stat No 73181588 match exactly. Gross weight 1674 kg matches. Origin Sweden verified via SE code in invoice Origin column.
non_blocking_observations: Certificate file contains both Original and Copy versions of the certificate, plus an embedded invoice copy on page 3. Verification performed using Original certificate (page 1) and separate invoice file (Document B).
Invoice Payer field label used instead of 'Invoice to'/'Bill to' – Payer is the primary billing party identifier in this invoice format.

output_quality_checks.consistency_verification: All 5 checkpoints are MATCH → comparison_result = IDENTICAL. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW checkpoints. Minimum confidence across checkpoints is 0.97 (goods_description) > 0.85 threshold. workflow_recommendation = AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE consistent with IDENTICAL.
